professional_summary.summary: I am a software engineer with experience across mobile development, backend systems, full stack web development, real-time event-driven systems, and hardware-connected software. I prefer backend engineering the most, but I can work across the stack when needed. I learn quickly, adapt to new domains fast, and focus on building useful systems with measurable impact.

btech.degree: Bachelor of Technology in Computer Science and Engineering

career_timeline.organization: Computer Society of India, Nirma University

work_experience.experience_summary: I work on a social media platform, building the feed ranking system and leading end-to-end development of a Causes module for cause-driven campaigns.
responsibilities: Built the feed ranking algorithm, using OpenSearch to index and score posts on per-user signals for personalized content
Leading end-to-end development of Causes, a module letting cause owners launch campaigns (beach cleanups, reforestation, etc.) with configurable donation, volunteer, and supporter workflows
Owned the PostgreSQL schema, backend, and REST APIs for the Causes module
key_learnings: Search-based ranking and relevance scoring with OpenSearch
End-to-end ownership of a product module
Designing PostgreSQL schemas and REST APIs for configurable workflows

work_experience.experience_summary: Built a marketing website for a print business as an independent freelancer.
responsibilities: Built a Next.js + Supabase marketing site for a print business
Implemented SEO-optimized product pages and a protected admin CMS
key_learnings: Building admin CMS workflows with Next.js and Supabase

work_experience.experience_summary: I worked on an intelligent exercise mat system and a full stack competition platform built for a competition called Burnhouse.
responsibilities: Designed and implemented exercise detection algorithms in Lua using sensor data
Improved algorithm accuracy so the mat became much harder to fool
Built a full stack competition platform using Next.js and Supabase for Burnhouse
Processed BLE event streams for real-time exercise tracking and leaderboards
Built a Lua-based multi-user UI flow using RFID or app-based events, retries, and time-based rules
Built an internal dashboard to upload LiDAR CSV logs, visualize sensor data, annotate anomalies, and export filtered datasets

ai_usage.positioning: Used as a productivity and code generation tool, not as a replacement for problem solving
details: I created detailed context documents for the exercise mat, sensors, and exercise logic
I designed the algorithms and the exercise detection rules myself
I used Claude Code to generate clean implementation faster
I kept ownership of logic, validation, decision making, and final quality

key_learnings: Learned Lua in a real production setting
Handled sensor-driven logic and strict exercise detection
Built real-time systems using BLE and Supabase
Worked across both low-level logic and full stack web development

work_experience.experience_summary: This was my first major production software experience. I worked on Medkart’s app revamp, learned Flutter from scratch, and delivered measurable product improvements.
onboarding_context: Observed POS usage, customer handling, billing, inventory flow, and business workflows

intern_evaluation_project.description: All 16 interns built a complete e-commerce website individually as an evaluation task
intern_evaluation_project.impact_on_career_direction: This project made me realize that backend engineering suited me more than frontend-heavy work

production_work: Worked on app revamp screens like Order page, Cart page, Search page, and Filter pages
Revamped 9 core app screens in 12 weeks
Worked on an app serving 50,000+ users
Built the Contact Us module end to end in Flutter
Implemented Flutter intents to reduce a 4-step call workflow to 1 tap
Implemented deep links so shared product links opened inside the app
Built skeleton loaders to improve perceived loading speed
Built a scalable sorting and filtering system with 5+ criteria
measurable_impact: Reduced customer support response time by 30%
Decreased bounce rates by 28% across 12+ pages
Reduced user actions by 75% for a store-owner calling workflow
key_learnings: Importance of understanding business context before coding
Adapting fast even when the assigned role was not the preferred one

responsibilities: Developed a responsive Drawer module for the CRM website
Worked with designers and backend developers for integration and functionality

application_ready_snippets.what_makes_me_exceptional: Within a short period of professional experience, I have delivered measurable impact across different domains, from reducing customer support response time by 30 percent and improving exercise detection speed by 55 percent, to building scalable backend foundations and real-time competition systems connected to hardware. I do well in fast-moving and unclear environments because I learn new technologies quickly, understand the real problem, and convert that understanding into working software.
application_ready_snippets.project_i_am_proud_of: At Hyperlab, I built a competition platform for Burnhouse, where multiple participants connected BLE-enabled exercise mats through their phones and competed in timed fitness challenges with live leaderboard updates. What made it special was the mix of hardware, Lua-based exercise logic, BLE events, backend updates, Supabase real-time flows, and a Next.js web interface working together as one system.
